page: Microsoft Azure E16as_v5 is an EASv5-series virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 128 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AES, and SHA_NI, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% of tested instances. Multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and cryptography execution fall within average parameters. Memory read bandwidth is stable, though uncached large-block write memory bandwidth is relatively weak, positioning in the bottom 25%. The instance demonstrates average throughput for static web serving, Redis SET operations, and small-to-medium language model inference.

This server does not include local storage, requiring external Premium Storage volumes for persistent data. The absence of complimentary public IPv4 addresses is another architectural consideration. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density relative to the vCPU count makes it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe drives.

The E16as_v5 is suited for memory-intensive applications, relational database management systems, caching layers like Redis, and enterprise applications requiring high RAM capacity without local storage overhead.
description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v5 is an EASv5-series virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 128 GB of RAM, providing a high 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio. This instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, and it lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, while multi-core operations, memory read bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. Conversely, large-block uncached write memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. Given its high memory density and dedicated CPU resources, this server is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications that rely on external storage solutions.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v5 is an EASv5 VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, optimized for memory-heavy database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for enterprise databases and caching.

128 GB of RAM providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
No local storage included, requiring external Premium Storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and memory-heavy workloads
